nodes.label: DQN Reward Stagnation Pattern (Apr 15-16 Runs)
nodes.description: Across all 10 runs on Apr 15-16, the rolling average reward stabilizes around -40000 with no upward trend. Neither the autopilot_continue series nor the fixing_staying_active series produced measurable improvement in final reward. High variance oscillations (~10000-20000 range) persist throughout training.

nodes.label: Staying Active Fix - No Visible Reward Improvement
nodes.description: The iterative fixes to the agent staying-active issue (v1 through v5, Apr 16) did not produce any visible change in training curve shape or final reward value. All v2-v5 runs show identical plateau dynamics around -40000. The fix may have addressed behavioral correctness but did not improve reward signal.

nodes.label: Initial Reward Spike Sign Change Between Run Groups
nodes.description: The autopilot_continue runs (2125, 2146, 2208) show a positive initial spike ~+60000 at episode 0, whereas the first autopilot_continue run (2105) shows a negative spike ~-150000 and the fixing_staying_active v2-v5 runs show a negative spike ~-150000. The fixing_staying_active v1 (1049) shows a moderate positive spike ~+25000. This sign change in the initial transient may reflect different checkpoint loading or environment initialization behavior between run groups.
nodes.norm_label: initial reward spike sign change between run groups
